question.content: In tobacco, self-incompatibility is controlled by multiple S alleles. A pollen tube is rejected when its S allele matches either S allele in the pistil, so only non-matching pollen can complete fertilization. For a diploid hermaphroditic population with 15 S alleles (S1 to S15), determine the maximum number of distinct genotypes that can exist. Answer format: <num>. Answer format: a single integer; write only the number itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Use the incompatibility rule: pollen tube growth is blocked when pollen S allele matches either pistil allele.
step_1.key_point: Matching S alleles are incompatible; non-matching S alleles are compatible.

step_3.content: In S1S2 selfing, both pollen types (S1 and S2) are blocked, so self-fertilization cannot produce offspring.
step_3.key_point: Selfing under this mechanism does not create genotypes in the next generation.

step_6.content: Therefore viable zygotes always combine two different S alleles, so only heterozygous genotypes are possible.
step_6.key_point: Count unordered pairs of distinct alleles from 15 options.

step_7.content: The maximum genotype count is the number of 2-combinations from 15 alleles.
